title: coronilla somalensis
intro: Coronilla somalensis, also known as Somalian crown vetch or bush vetch, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Somalian and Ethiopian folk medicine, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and alleviating menstrual disorders. Coronilla somalensis is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat a range of health issues.
